commit_message:
[CI] ShardSearchPhaseAPMMetricsTests testTimeRangeFilterAllResults failing (#138054)

Closes #137979.

We are only determining the search request attributes once for a given
CanMatchNodeRequest for the can-match phase duration metric for all
of the shards in that request using the first shard. If the first shard
processed is empty, we will not have the time range attribute since we
need to parse the search terms to obtain that attribute and we skip
parsing since the shard is empty.

Since we do not want to add additional overhead by obtaining the search
request attributes for each shard separately and empty shards are fairly
rare in practice, I have modified the failing test to handle this edge case. I have
also added an additional test case for non-empty shards for the non-edge
case.


---------

Co-authored-by: elasticsearchmachine <infra-root+elasticsearchmachine@elastic.co>

commit_message:
[Inference API] Address AuthorizationTaskExecutorIT failures (#144312)

This commit addresses failures in `AuthorizationTaskExecutorIT`.

  1. test `testCreatesChatCompletion_AndThenCreatesTextEmbedding` now
  waits for the second endpoint to be created. As the second endpoint
  is discovered by the `AuthorizationPoller` we need to wait for the new
  endpoint to be stored.
  2. tests based on `testEndpointGetsUpdated_GivenFingerprintChanged` now
  wait for inference system indices to be green (have their shards initialized)
  after the endpoint is stored. The latter guarantees the indices would have
  been created so that the `ensureGreen` call works as expected.

Closes #138012
Closes #144249

Code assumes that there are doc value skippers for a field in every segment and that may not be true.
commit_message:
Address issue with DateFieldMapper#isFieldWithinQuery(...) (#138032)

If a date field has doc values skippers enabled, then it isn't guaranteed that every segment will actually have skippers. The logic was currently assuming that it did.

Closes #138024

issue_title: [ES|QL] Remove the implicit `LIMIT` command added to each subquery (`UnionAll` branch)
issue_body:
This is a follow up to https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/135744, removing the implicit `LIMIT` command added to each subquery potentially lifts the restrictions on:

1. Subqueries followed by `LOOKUP JOIN` under CCS context, as `LOOKUP JOIN` may not work with [remote index patterns + `LIMIT`](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/main/x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/esql/plan/logical/join/Join.java) before it.
2. Subqueries followed by `INLINE STATS`, as `INLINE STATS` may not work when there is a [`LIMIT`](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/main/x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/esql/analysis/Verifier.java#L269) command before it.

commit_message:
Ensure IndicesOptions are mutated for GetInferenceFieldsActionRequestTests (#138163)

When `IndicesOptions` are mutated, we may produce `null`. The request constructor
sets indices options to the default ones when the argument is `null`.
Thus, if the original instance had the default `IndicesOptions` to begin with,
we will end up with a mutation that is identical.

This fixes this by generating a new random `IndicesOptions` object in the case above.

Closed #138134

commit_message:
Fix `DataStreamLifecycleDownsampleIT.testUpdateDownsampleSamplingMode` (#138174)

It was clear from failed tests that DLM was still progressing when the
timeout of 10s was reached. Since DLM works on a poll interval (set to
1s in these tests), there's not much we can do to speed up the test.
Therefore, we simply bump the timeout from 10s to 30s. This should give
DLM enough time to complete the downsampling mode change.

Note that `safeAwait` uses a timeout of 10s and `awaitClusterState` uses
a timeout of 30s; that's why there's no explicit timeout change.

Fixes #138135

issue_title: nested KNN query returns no results when using nested query on top level field as filter
commit_message:
Auto prefiltering for queries on dense semantic_text fields (#138989)

`knn` queries allow specifying `filters` that will be applied before
the knn search. This `pre-filtering` allows the `knn` to return `k`
results. If such filters are to applied only after the `knn` executes,
then the `knn` returns the `k` matching results but the filters can
filter out some of them thus potentially returning fewer than `k` results.

`semantic_text` fields can be queried with:

DSL

- `match` queries
- `semantic` queries
- `knn` queries

ES|QL

- `match` queries
- `knn` queries

For DSL, `knn` queries allow users to specify direct prefilters.
However, `match` and `semantic` queries provide no way to do so.
Same goes for ES|QL `match`. Noting that ES|QL `KNN` already implements
auto pre-filtering where conjunctions are pushed down to the `knn` query
as prefilters.

This commit implements semantic_text auto pre-filtering for `semantic_text` queries
in DSL (`match` and `semantic` queries) and ES|QL (`MATCH`).

We achieve this by adding an `AutoPrefilteringScope` object to the
`SearchExecutionContext`. When we convert a `bool` query to a lucene query,
we push its `must`, `filter`, and `must_not` clauses to the `AutoPrefilteringScope`.
At that stage queries have already been rewritten. Semantic queries using
`text_embedding` inference endpoints are rewritten to knn vector queries
that are auto-prefiltering enabled. Then, when an auto-prefiltering
enabled knn vector query is converted to its lucene equivalent, we
fetch the prefilters from the `SearchExecutionContext` and we apply
them to the knn vector query - which supports pre-filtering already.

ES|QL queries that contain `MATCH` automatically benefit from this implementation because
they are rewritten in `bool` queries.

Limitations

DSL

- nested queries are excluded from pre-filtering (#138184)

ES|QL

- filters that are not translatable to lucene queries will be applied as post-filters

Relates #132068

issue_title: Reindex metrics can be inaccurate with slicing enabled
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.16+ (Reindex metrics [introduced in 8.16](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/111845))

### Problem Description

[Reindex metrics](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/main/modules/reindex/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/reindex/ReindexMetrics.java) are currently emitted per slice if slicing is enabled, and emitted per whole operation if slicing is disabled. This leads to confusion because the meaning of metric like `es.reindex.duration.histogram` are different based on whether slicing is used in a reindex operation. It also leads to inaccurate metrics as the metrics for per operation and per slice are mixed together.

### Steps to Reproduce

Add slicing (eg. `reindex().setSlices(5).source("source")...`) in [this test](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/aa955f797803dd6cb73afda3bcd88bef1cbc592e/modules/reindex/src/internalClusterTest/java/org/elasticsearch/index/reindex/ReindexPluginMetricsIT.java#L156) will cause the test to fail with 5 metric counts instead of 1.
commit_message: Add slicing mode and fix operation-level reindex metrics (#143415)

Data frame analytics assigns ml__incremental_id during reindex in source
scan order. The train/test splitter consumes Random in incremental-id
order, so non-deterministic reindex order produced different training
sets for the same randomize_seed (flaky RegressionIT).

Sort the reindex search by _doc, consistent with other ML bulk scroll
paths. Unmute RegressionIT.testTwoJobsWithSameRandomizeSeedUseSameTrainingSet.

Closes #138319

commit_message:
ESQL: Prevent circular alias references in `DeduplicateAggs` (#139175)

## Summary

Fixes circular reference detection in alias chains during
`DeduplicateAggs` optimizations.

## Problem

The `PushDownEnrich` optimization can create circular references when
pushing `Enrich` past `Project` operations, causing
`ql_illegal_argument_exception: Potential cycle detected` errors when
`DeduplicateAggs`.

Example query:

```esql
FROM languages_lookup 
| RENAME language_name AS message  
| ENRICH languages_policy ON language_code 
| STATS a = count(to_lower(language_name)), b = count(language_name)
```

## Changes

Skip aliases that would create circular references by checking if
`alias.toAttribute()` equals the resolved `alias.child()`.

Closes #138346 Closes #139541

commit_message:
Tolerate unavailable ML stats shards when loading DFA progress (#145272)

Start data frame analytics loads stored progress via GetDataFrameAnalyticsStats
before cardinality validation. Multi-search against .ml-stats-* could fail
with NoShardAvailableActionException while shards are not assigned yet,
surfacing a generic search error instead of the expected validation message.

Treat shard-unavailable failures on ML stats/state indices like missing
stats documents. Unmute MlWithSecurityIT YAML test (issue #138409).

Made-with: Cursor

issue_title: knn query on semantic_text field with semantic_text filter fails
commit_message:
Intercept filters to knn queries (#138457)

`knn` queries can have filter queries. Those filters may contain
semantic queries (e.g. `knn`, `sparse_vector`, or `match` queries
targetting `semantic_text` fields). We also need to intercept those
in order to perform inference for their `query` field during the
coordinator node rewrite.

This commit achieves this with the following changes:

- `SemanticKnnVectorQueryRewriteInterceptor` attempts to rewrite filter queries.
This means we rely on a rewrite cycle to attempt to intercept every query down the
tree of each filter query.

- `InterceptedInferenceQueryBuilder` now has a `customCoordinatorNodeRewrite` method
that subclasses can implement to implement additional rewriting logic needed, e.g. rewrite
inner queries.

- `InterceptedInferenceKnnVectorQueryBuilder` implements `customCoordinatorNodeRewrite` so
that the filter queries are rewritten.

This commit fixes the exceptions throws in #138410. However, searches that contain semantic text
queries as filters to a semantic text knn query will return `0` hits due to another issue that
is captured in #138184.

Closes #138410

commit_message:
[Inference API] Set MockResponse body at runtime to prevent race (#141027)

This commit adds the ability to set the body of a MockResponse at
runtime to allow the content of the body to be determined by the
MockRequest that is supposed to generate the response. This prevents a
race condition where responses were not being paired up with their
intended requests due to the requests being sent in parallel.

Closes #138451
Closes #140890

commit_message:
Extend timeout in `IngestGeoIpClientYamlTestSuiteIT` (#138610)

Some slow CI test clusters are not able to download all four GeoIP databases within 10 seconds, so we bump the timeout to 20 seconds.

See #138460 for a more detailed analysis.

Fixes #138460
Fixes #138502
